articles.title: Why US and China are struggling to reach a consensus over future shape of relationship
articles.summary: The ongoing struggle between the US and China to define a "constructive" relationship underscores significant geopolitical tensions, as both nations grapple with deep-seated differences that threaten strategic stability and the potential for future collaboration. This uncertainty not only impacts bilateral relations but also has far-reaching implications for global diplomacy and security.

articles.title: Beijing sends new coastguard force into waters east of Taiwan
articles.summary: Beijing's deployment of a new coastguard task force east of Taiwan signals a strategic escalation in regional tensions, particularly following diplomatic overtures to the Philippines, highlighting China's assertive stance in contested waters and its implications for U.S.-China relations. This maneuver underscores the delicate balance of power in the region and the potential for increased conflict over sovereignty issues.
